name: State Reconciliation
description: A pattern where components maintain a desired state of the world and an actual state of the world, and continuously reconcile these states to ensure that the system is in the desired configuration.

name: Event-Driven Architecture
description: A pattern where components react to events or changes from external sources, such as API requests, pod lifecycle events, or etcd status responses, and trigger actions based on these events.

name: Exponential Backoff
description: A pattern used in failure handling where components retry operations with increasing intervals to handle transient failures, reducing the load on the system and improving robustness.

name: State Management
description: A pattern where components manage internal state representations, such as desired and actual states, to track and control the lifecycle of resources or operations.

name: Webhook Integration
description: A pattern where components integrate with external webhooks for extended functionality, such as admission control or policy enforcement, and handle responses from these webhooks.
